stories.title: Sen. Lee urges Trump to recall Congress over DHS shutdown
stories.description: The Utah Republican wants the president to invoke a constitutional power unused since 1948 to end the record 49-day DHS funding lapse.

stories.title: S&P 500 caps five-week losing streak amid Iran war and jobs surprise
stories.description: U.S. stocks closed Thursday slightly lower as oil topped $110 a barrel and a stronger-than-expected March jobs report dimmed hopes for rate cuts.

stories.title: Brent crude hits $141 as Hormuz closure fuels worst energy crisis since 1970s
stories.description: Oil prices surged to their highest level since 2008 this week as analysts warn strategic reserves will be exhausted by mid-April without a reopening of the strait.

stories.title: Iran launches drone strikes on US bases in UAE, Kuwait
stories.description: The Arash-2 drone attacks mark a further escalation as the US-Israeli war against Iran enters its sixth week.

stories.title: Pope Leo urges world not to 'grow numb' to war at Easter vigil
stories.description: The first American pope led his first Easter Vigil at St. Peter's Basilica, calling on Catholics to resist paralysis and work for peace.

stories.title: Artemis II crew prepares for Monday lunar flyby
stories.description: On their fourth day in space, the four astronauts are more than halfway to the Moon and readying for a record-breaking flyby.

stories.title: US wage growth hits slowest pace since 2021 as Iran war stokes inflation fears
stories.description: Annual earnings growth for production workers fell to 3.4% in March, well below the 4.2% inflation rate the OECD projects for 2026.

stories.title: WHO warns of humanitarian crisis as Iran hospitals come under attack
stories.description: WHO has verified over 20 attacks on healthcare in Iran since March 1, as strikes continue to cripple medical infrastructure across the country.

stories.title: White House calls press lid as Trump health rumors spread online
stories.description: Unverified speculation surged after the president's schedule showed only "Executive Time" and no briefing on the Iran military crisis.

stories.title: U.N. Security Council postpones vote on Strait of Hormuz resolution to next week
stories.description: The Bahrain-backed measure was watered down to permit only defensive actions after Russia, China, and France opposed authorizing military force.

stories.title: Russia, Ukraine trade deadly strikes as Zelenskyy meets Erdogan
stories.description: Five people were killed and dozens wounded in a Russian drone strike on a market in Nikopol as Zelenskyy held peace-focused talks in Istanbul.

stories.title: Israel readying strikes on Iran's energy sites, awaits US approval
stories.description: A senior Israeli defense official said strikes could come within the next week, as Trump's April 6 deadline for Iran to reopen the Strait of Hormuz nears.

stories.title: Zelenskyy, Erdogan agree on new security steps at Istanbul talks
stories.description: Ukraine will share wartime expertise and technology with Turkey as part of a broader cooperation deal reached Saturday.

stories.title: Displaced Lebanese Shiites face sectarian hostility amid war
stories.description: Over 1.1 million people have fled Israeli strikes, but many Shiite families encounter discrimination, inflated rents, and eviction demands in host communities.

stories.title: Trump administration appeals to resume White House ballroom work
stories.description: The emergency motion argues the halted $400 million project has left the executive mansion "open and exposed," posing security risks.

stories.title: Israel hits Beirut, Tyre as Lebanon war enters second month
stories.description: Strikes damaged a hospital in Tyre and targeted Iranian Quds Force positions in Beirut as the conflict's death toll surpasses 1,368.

stories.title: India confirms Iranian oil purchases amid Hormuz crisis
stories.description: The petroleum ministry said refiners have secured crude and LPG supplies, marking India's first Iranian oil shipment since 2019.

stories.title: Trump's $1.5T defense budget draws fire from both parties
stories.description: Sen. Collins joined Democrats in criticizing the proposal, citing insufficient Navy funding and cuts to domestic programs she called "proven."

stories.title: ICE arrests Soleimani's niece after Rubio revokes green cards
stories.description: Federal agents detained Hamideh Soleimani Afshar and her daughter in Los Angeles after the secretary of state terminated their permanent resident status.

stories.title: Iran authorizes humanitarian ships through Strait of Hormuz
stories.description: The directive permits vessels carrying essential goods and livestock feed to reach Iranian ports, over five weeks into an effective blockade of the critical waterway.

stories.title: Trump gives Iran 48 hours as strikes hit petrochemical sites
stories.description: The president reaffirmed his April 6 deadline for Tehran to reopen the Strait of Hormuz, warning "all hell" would follow if it does not comply.

stories.title: Witkoff and Kushner plan first visit to Kyiv after Easter
stories.description: Ukraine's Budanov says the U.S. envoys are expected to travel to Kyiv after Orthodox Easter on April 12, their first trip to the capital.

stories.title: Thailand probes fuel hoarding as Southeast Asia's energy crisis deepens
stories.description: Five weeks after Iran closed the Strait of Hormuz, Southeast Asian nations face mounting fuel shortages, soaring costs, and emergency rationing measures.

stories.title: Iran rapidly repairing bombed missile sites, U.S. intel says
stories.description: Intelligence assessments show Iranian forces restoring struck bunkers within hours, even as Trump projects the war will end in weeks.

stories.title: Erdogan tells NATO chief Iran war has caused 'geostrategic impasse'
stories.description: The Turkish president urged the international community to intensify efforts to end the conflict during a Saturday phone call with Mark Rutte.

stories.title: Slovak, Hungarian PMs demand EU lift Russia energy sanctions
stories.description: The two leaders cited Europe's deepening energy crisis, driven by the Iran conflict, as reason to restore Russian oil and gas flows immediately.

stories.title: Former Carter aide calls US a 'rogue nation' over Iran war
stories.description: Gary Sick, who served on the National Security Council under three presidents, warned the US is "flouting the laws of war" as the conflict enters its sixth week.

stories.title: Hamas accuses Israel of fabricating threats to justify Gaza ceasefire violations
stories.description: Since the October ceasefire took effect, Israeli attacks have killed at least 713 Palestinians, according to Gaza's Health Ministry.

stories.title: Gaza ceasefire talks end in Cairo with plans to resume next week
stories.description: Hamas called for the immediate formation of a transitional committee to govern Gaza and oversee reconstruction under the ceasefire's second phase.

stories.title: Iran downs 2 US warplanes, undercutting Trump's victory claims
stories.description: The shootdowns mark the first time manned U.S. aircraft have been brought down since the war began, with one crew member still missing.

stories.title: Interception debris strikes Oracle building in Dubai amid Iran conflict
stories.description: Oracle is among 18 U.S. tech firms that Iran's Revolutionary Guard declared "legitimate targets" in late March.

stories.title: Iran says it 'never refused' Pakistan-hosted talks with US
stories.description: Iran's foreign minister pushed back on reports that Tehran rejected mediation, even as Pakistan denied its diplomatic initiative had stalled.

stories.title: Iran vows to 'finish' any US ground operation as war nears 6th week
stories.description: A diplomatic source said Tehran would determine the outcome of any American ground incursion, adding that no negotiations are underway.

stories.title: Turkey confirms second ship crosses Strait of Hormuz
stories.description: Omani tankers also charted a new southern route bypassing Iran's controlled corridor as transit volumes inch up from wartime lows.

stories.title: UAE intercepts 79 Iranian projectiles as strikes batter Gulf infrastructure
stories.description: Saturday's wave brought total interceptions since Feb. 28 to nearly 2,700, as key energy and industrial sites sustain growing damage.

stories.title: UAE rejects Zarif's peace proposal, demands Iran compensate Gulf states
stories.description: Dr. Anwar Gargash called the former Iranian FM's plan "fundamentally flawed" for ignoring Tehran's missile and drone attacks on Arab neighbors.

stories.title: Trump weighs broader cabinet shake-up as war frustration mounts
stories.description: Lutnick, Gabbard, and Chavez-DeRemer are among those on thin ice as Trump's approval hits a term low amid the Iran conflict.

stories.title: Ukrainian drone strikes kill one in Taganrog, hit chemical plants deep in Russia
stories.description: Overnight attacks targeted southern Russia's port city and industrial sites over 750 km from the front lines, leaving four in critical condition.

stories.title: Five EU finance ministers demand windfall tax on energy firms
stories.description: Germany, Italy, Spain, Portugal, and Austria urged the European Commission to tax energy companies' excess profits from the Iran war price surge.

stories.title: Trump's FY2027 budget boosts defense 42%, flat-funds SSA
stories.description: The $2.2 trillion proposal cuts nondefense spending by $73 billion while avoiding reforms to Social Security and Medicare as insolvency looms.

stories.title: US pulled 1,500 sailors from Bahrain naval base after Iranian strikes
stories.description: Relief groups have distributed over $1 million to evacuated families who fled with little more than backpacks, NPR reported.

stories.title: North Korea razes villages near key missile launch site
stories.description: Satellite imagery shows hundreds of buildings demolished near the Sohae station, pointing to an expansion of the facility amid an accelerating weapons buildup.

stories.title: GOP cracks widen over Iran war as midterms loom
stories.description: Rising gas prices, casualties, and an approaching War Powers Resolution deadline fuel Republican defections from Trump on Iran.

stories.title: US Embassy urges Americans to leave Lebanon over Iran university threats
stories.description: The Beirut embassy warned Friday that Iran and allied militias may target universities in Lebanon amid ongoing airstrikes and rocket fire.

stories.title: French, Japanese ships cross Hormuz in first Western transits since Iran war
stories.description: CMA CGM and Mitsui O.S.K. Lines vessels broke through Iran's selective blockade this week, though roughly 3,000 ships remain stranded globally.

stories.title: Doctors Without Borders confirms Gaza supply shutdown since January
stories.description: Doctors Without Borders warned that Israeli restrictions have left nearly half of essential chronic medications critically low across Gaza.

stories.title: Iran drone strike on Riyadh embassy far worse than disclosed
stories.description: Multiple embassy floors were heavily damaged and sections remain "unrecoverable," according to current and former U.S. officials.

stories.title: Earthquake kills 8 in Afghanistan, tremors felt across South Asia
stories.description: A magnitude 5.9 quake centered in the Hindu Kush region caused a house to collapse on the outskirts of Kabul, killing an entire family.

stories.title: Three charged over arson attack on Jewish ambulances in London
stories.description: The defendants are due to appear at Westminster Magistrates' Court on Saturday after being arrested on April 1.

stories.title: Pentagon logs 365 troops wounded in Operation Epic Fury
stories.description: The Defense Department added the Iran campaign to its official casualty database on Friday, providing the most detailed public accounting of the war's toll.

stories.title: War silences Good Friday in Jerusalem as Pope Leo XIV calls for peace
stories.description: Only a handful of worshippers walked the Via Dolorosa as Israeli restrictions blocked access to holy sites amid the U.S.-Israel war on Iran.

stories.title: Trump signs memo ordering back pay for all DHS employees
stories.description: The directive extends relief beyond TSA workers to roughly 220,000 federal employees who have gone without pay during the 48-day partial shutdown.

stories.title: US intel finds Iran unlikely to negotiate on Hormuz blockade
stories.description: Intelligence assessments say Tehran sees no need to comply with U.S. demands as the April 6 deadline to reopen the strait approaches.

stories.title: Justice Alito was secretly hospitalized in March, court confirms
stories.description: The 76-year-old Supreme Court justice was treated for dehydration after falling ill at a Federalist Society dinner in Philadelphia.

stories.title: New acting AG Blanche says Epstein files should not be DOJ priority
stories.description: Democrats and Republicans vow to enforce a subpoena requiring fired AG Pam Bondi to testify on April 14 about unreleased Epstein documents.

stories.title: UN says Iran war is accelerating global renewable energy shift
stories.description: IRENA reports renewables hit a record 49.4% of global capacity in 2025 as the energy crisis pushes countries toward domestic clean power.

stories.title: Oracle faces backlash over H-1B visa filings amid mass layoffs
stories.description: Critics call it a "slap in the face" that the company filed over 3,100 foreign-worker visa petitions in the same period it cut up to 30,000 jobs.

stories.title: 23 state AGs sue to block Trump's mail voting order
stories.description: The coalition filed suit Friday in Boston, arguing the executive order unconstitutionally strips states of their authority to administer elections.

stories.title: Satellite images confirm Iran damaged $136M US radar in Saudi Arabia
stories.description: New imagery shows charring and missing sections on a THAAD radar antenna struck at Prince Sultan Air Base on March 1.

stories.title: IDF says disarming Hezbollah 'not a goal' of Lebanon campaign
stories.description: A senior military official's remarks signal a shift from earlier war aims after one month of fighting that has hit over 3,500 targets.

stories.title: Trump budget seeks to privatize TSA screening
stories.description: The fiscal 2027 proposal pairs a $1.5 trillion defense request with a 10% cut to nondefense spending, including a push to shift airport security to private contractors.

stories.title: US adds 178,000 jobs in March, far beating forecasts
stories.description: The unemployment rate fell to 4.3% as the labor market rebounded sharply from February's loss of 92,000 jobs.

stories.title: Macron calls for 'coalition of independence' against US and China
stories.description: Speaking in Seoul, the French president urged democratic middle powers to resist becoming "vassals of two hegemonic powers."

stories.title: Judge reaffirms block on DOJ subpoenas targeting Fed's Powell
stories.description: The ruling sets up a likely appeal that could delay Trump's push to replace Powell with Kevin Warsh before Powell's term expires May 15.

stories.title: China's Tianlong-3 rocket fails on maiden flight
stories.description: The privately built rocket, designed to rival SpaceX's Falcon 9, suffered a mid-flight anomaly launching from the Gobi Desert.

stories.title: Georgia legislature adjourns without resolving voting machine standoff
stories.description: Conflicting state laws take effect July 1, leaving all 159 counties without a clear path for conducting November's midterm elections.

stories.title: Russia holds nuclear missile drills as Trump threatens NATO exit
stories.description: Moscow showcased Yars ICBMs in Siberian exercises as European leaders warned Trump's withdrawal threats serve Putin's interests.

stories.title: Environmental groups sue over Gulf drilling's ESA exemption
stories.description: Conservation groups filed lawsuits challenging the Trump administration's unprecedented decision to exempt all Gulf oil and gas operations from the Endangered Species Act.

stories.title: Global food prices jump for second straight month as Middle East war disrupts supply chains
stories.description: The FAO Food Price Index rose sharply in March, driven by surging energy and fertilizer costs linked to the conflict in Iran.

stories.title: Trump names Vance 'fraud czar,' targeting Democratic states
stories.description: The announcement follows a major hospice fraud crackdown in Los Angeles that led to 11 charges and 221 provider suspensions.

stories.title: Min Aung Hlaing wins parliamentary vote to become Myanmar president
stories.description: The former coup leader won a bicameral vote Friday after resigning as military chief, completing a five-year transition to nominal civilian rule.

stories.title: South Korea, France elevate ties to strategic partnership
stories.description: The two leaders signed 11 MOUs covering nuclear energy, critical minerals, and defense after summit talks in Seoul on Friday.

stories.title: Trump says Israel will halt Iran war on his orders
stories.description: In a TIME interview, the president claimed personal authority over the five-week-old conflict, saying of Israel: "They'll do what I tell them."

stories.title: Shell, TotalEnergies and BP eye majority stake in Gulf deepwater field
stories.description: Beacon Offshore Energy and HEQ Deepwater have launched a sale of their combined 51% stake in the Shenandoah ultra-deepwater project, sources say.

stories.title: Hegseth forces out Army chief of staff Gen. Randy George
stories.description: The dismissal caps a turbulent week after Hegseth blocked promotions of Black and female officers, deepening rifts with Army leadership.

stories.title: McConnell collapses at Senate Republican luncheon
stories.description: The 84-year-old Kentucky senator required medical assistance Thursday, the latest in a string of health scares during his final term.

stories.title: IRGC launches fresh strikes on steel, aluminum plants across Middle East
stories.description: Iran announced a new wave of attacks on April 2 targeting U.S.-linked steel and aluminum facilities, escalating a conflict that has sent prices soaring.

stories.title: Vance to visit Hungary, meet with Orban days before election
stories.description: The White House confirmed the vice president will travel to Budapest on April 7-8 as polls show Orban trailing his challenger by double digits.

stories.title: Dem senators urge SEC, Pentagon to probe insider trading
stories.description: Sens. Warner and Schiff called on regulators Thursday to investigate suspicious trades ahead of major Trump administration policy moves.

stories.title: Boeser's hat trick lifts last-place Canucks past Avalanche 8-6
stories.description: Nathan MacKinnon scored his 50th goal of the season, but Vancouver rallied to snap a six-game skid in a wild game in Denver.

stories.title: GCC chief condemns Iran strikes in first-ever UN Council address
stories.description: Jassim Albudaiwi warned the world is at a "dangerous crossroads" and urged the Security Council to enforce Resolution 2817.

stories.title: OpenAI acquires TBPN, Silicon Valley's hit tech talk show
stories.description: The deal extends OpenAI's reach into media, marking a departure from its recent string of developer-focused startup acquisitions.

stories.title: IRGC claims new strikes on Amazon data center in Bahrain
stories.description: Iran's military says it targeted the AWS cloud computing center as part of the 90th wave of Operation True Promise 4.

stories.title: Blue Owl caps fund redemptions after $5.4B in withdrawal requests
stories.description: Investors sought to pull over 40% of one fund's shares, the worst hit yet in a wave of redemption pressure sweeping the private credit sector.

stories.title: US jobless claims fall to 202,000, defying Iran war headwinds
stories.description: Weekly filings dropped well below forecasts even as the conflict's energy shock weighs on the broader economy and hiring outlook.

stories.title: Magnitude 7.4 earthquake kills one in Indonesia, triggers brief tsunami warning
stories.description: The undersea quake toppled buildings across northern Indonesia and generated small tsunami waves before warnings were lifted hours later.

stories.title: Iraq's oil exports halt as Hormuz closure cuts output 70%
stories.description: Southern Iraq's oil production has plunged from 3.1 million to 900,000 barrels per day, with exports completely stopped due to the Strait of Hormuz shutdown.

stories.title: One year after Liberation Day, tariff refunds loom
stories.description: April 2 marks the anniversary of Trump's sweeping tariffs, most of which were struck down by the Supreme Court, with $165 billion in refunds still pending.

stories.title: Syracuse University to close or pause 93 programs
stories.description: The provost said 93 of roughly 460 degree programs and certificates will be eliminated, affecting about 20% of offerings and 258 students.

stories.title: CDC pauses rabies, monkeypox, and other disease testing amid staff cuts
stories.description: The agency suspended more than two dozen types of diagnostic testing, citing a routine review as staffing fell by up to 25%.

stories.title: Trump leaves Supreme Court early, calls birthright citizenship 'stupid'
stories.description: The president became the first to attend oral arguments, but justices signaled skepticism of his order to end birthright citizenship.

stories.title: US troop housing in Gulf hotels may violate laws of war
stories.description: Legal experts say relocating troops from damaged bases to civilian hotels blurs the line between military and civilian targets under the Geneva Conventions.

stories.title: Nepal charges 32 in $20M Everest helicopter rescue scam
stories.description: Guides allegedly poisoned trekkers with baking soda to trigger fake evacuations, then forged medical and flight records to defraud insurers.

stories.title: DHS scraps Noem's $100K contract approval rule, unblocking FEMA relief
stories.description: Secretary Markwayne Mullin's first major action reverses a policy that delayed over 1,000 FEMA contracts and billions in disaster aid.

stories.title: Mangione's state and federal trials both delayed
stories.description: A New York judge pushed the state murder trial to September, hours after a federal judge moved jury selection to October, compressing an already tight timeline.

stories.title: Russia vows continued aid to Cuba after first oil shipment in three months
stories.description: Moscow called Cuba its "closest ally in the Caribbean" as Havana faces a deepening energy crisis under a U.S. fuel blockade.

stories.title: Judge lets ABA lawsuit over Trump's law firm orders proceed
stories.description: A federal judge denied the DOJ's motion to dismiss, ruling the ABA plausibly showed Trump's executive orders suppressed free expression.

stories.title: ICE says it bought phone-hacking spyware to fight fentanyl
stories.description: The agency's disclosure to lawmakers is its first public justification for a controversial $2 million contract with Israeli spyware firm Paragon Solutions.

stories.title: US lifts sanctions on Venezuela's acting president Rodríguez
stories.description: The Treasury Department delisted Rodríguez, who had been sanctioned since 2018, as Washington deepens ties with Caracas after Maduro's capture.

stories.title: Appeals court blocks HUD from overhauling homelessness grants
stories.description: The 1st Circuit upheld an injunction preserving billions in permanent housing funds, rejecting the Trump administration's bid to impose new restrictions.

stories.title: Seizing Iran's uranium by force would risk casualties, experts warn
stories.description: A military operation to secure Iran's 440.9 kg of near-weapons-grade uranium could require 1,000 troops and face radiation hazards, former officials say.

stories.title: Shell in advanced talks to develop major gas fields in Venezuela
stories.description: The energy giant is pursuing four offshore areas near Trinidad with combined reserves of roughly 20 trillion cubic feet, according to Reuters.

stories.title: Iran's Pezeshkian tells Americans his country 'harbors no enmity' toward them
stories.description: The open letter bypasses the Trump administration to appeal directly to the U.S. public as the military conflict enters its fifth week.

stories.title: AOC pledges to oppose all U.S. arms funding for Israel
stories.description: The commitment, made at a DSA endorsement forum Tuesday night, reverses her July 2025 vote to protect Iron Dome funding.

stories.title: Israel kills Hezbollah's Southern Front commander in Beirut naval strike
stories.description: Hajj Yusuf Ismail Hashem, a 40-year Hezbollah veteran who led operations in southern Lebanon, died in an overnight strike on the Jnah district.

stories.title: Macron reaffirms France won't join US-Israel war on Iran
stories.description: Speaking in Tokyo, Macron pushed back on Trump's accusation that France blocked U.S. military flights, saying France was never consulted.

stories.title: South Korea exports hit record $86B as global factories stockpile amid Iran war
stories.description: Semiconductor shipments surged over 150%, while euro zone and U.S. factory activity beat forecasts as firms panic-bought materials ahead of further disruptions.

stories.title: Qubits needed to crack encryption fall 200-fold in under a year
stories.description: Three papers have slashed the estimated quantum computing power needed to break RSA-2048 from 20 million qubits to under 100,000, accelerating the threat to modern encryption.

stories.title: Trump to address nation on Iran as war enters day 33
stories.description: The president said Tuesday that U.S. forces would be "leaving soon," predicting the conflict could end within two to three weeks.

stories.title: Macron and Takaichi align on Hormuz crisis, rare earths in Tokyo summit
stories.description: The first French presidential visit to Japan in over a decade was dominated by the Middle East conflict, energy security, and reducing reliance on China for critical minerals.

stories.title: Parker Solar Probe data challenges models of Sun's magnetic engine
stories.description: Protons and heavy ions behave differently during solar magnetic reconnection, contradicting models that assumed uniform particle acceleration.

stories.title: Conservative justices question Trump birthright order
stories.description: Trump became the first sitting president to attend Supreme Court oral arguments as justices from both wings challenged his executive order.

stories.title: USTR flags Canadian alcohol bans, 'Buy Canadian' as trade barriers
stories.description: The annual report demands American liquor "immediately and permanently" return to provincial shelves ahead of the USMCA's first formal review in July.

stories.title: New papers slash qubit estimates for codebreaking
stories.description: The March 31 papers sharply cut projected quantum resources needed to crack elliptic curve encryption, intensifying pressure to adopt post-quantum safeguards.

stories.title: ICE agents to remain at airports indefinitely, Duffy says
stories.description: Transportation secretary says deployment will continue as TSA struggles to rebuild staffing after the DHS shutdown left officers unpaid for weeks.

stories.title: Forest Service to move headquarters to Salt Lake City in sweeping overhaul
stories.description: The USDA announced the agency's nine regional offices will close and be replaced by 15 state directors under a new organizational model.

stories.title: China and Pakistan propose ceasefire as Chinese ships transit Hormuz
stories.description: Beijing confirmed three Chinese vessels crossed the strait after coordinating with Tehran, as China and Pakistan unveiled a five-point peace plan.

stories.title: DHS pauses warehouse purchases for immigrant detention
stories.description: New Secretary Mullin is reviewing the $38.3 billion detention expansion program launched by his predecessor, Kristi Noem.

stories.title: Netanyahu says Israel destroying Iran's weapons capability
stories.description: In a Passover eve address, the prime minister said Israel had inflicted "10 plagues" on Iran and its regional proxy networks.

stories.title: Syria vows to stay out of US-Israeli war on Iran unless attacked
stories.description: President Ahmed al-Sharaa declared neutrality in a London speech, saying Syria has "had enough war" as the conflict enters its second month.

stories.title: Rubio directs US embassies to use X and military psyops against propaganda
stories.description: The diplomatic cable instructs staff worldwide to coordinate with Pentagon psychological operations and leverage Elon Musk's platform.

stories.title: Judge blocks deal that would let churches endorse candidates
stories.description: A federal judge in Texas rejected a proposed IRS settlement that would have exempted houses of worship from the Johnson Amendment's ban on political endorsements.

stories.title: Explosions rock Damascus as Israel intercepts Iranian missiles over Syria
stories.description: Syrian state TV reported blasts across the capital on Tuesday as Israeli air defenses shot down Iranian projectiles crossing Syrian airspace.

stories.title: FDA moves to allow banned peptides backed by RFK Jr.
stories.description: The agency is expected to reclassify more than a dozen injectable peptides restricted in 2023, restoring access through compounding pharmacies.

stories.title: Judge orders UPenn to hand over Jewish employee records
stories.description: The ruling gives Penn until May 1 to comply with the subpoena but bars disclosure of individuals' ties to specific Jewish organizations.

stories.title: Majority disapprove of Trump's signature on dollar bills, YouGov poll finds
stories.description: The survey found 48% strongly oppose replacing the U.S. Treasurer's signature with the president's on new paper currency.

stories.title: Bipartisan senators introduce bill to boost Taiwan drone production
stories.description: The legislation would create a U.S.-Taiwan working group to co-produce drones and build supply chains independent from China.

stories.title: Supreme Court strikes down Colorado conversion therapy ban in 8-1 ruling
stories.description: The decision in Chiles v. Salazar requires courts to apply heightened First Amendment scrutiny to laws restricting talk therapy for minors.

stories.title: Judge permanently blocks Trump order defunding NPR, PBS
stories.description: U.S. District Judge Randolph Moss ruled the executive order violated the First Amendment by punishing the outlets over speech the president dislikes.

stories.title: Vance to publish book on Catholic faith while in office
stories.description: "Communion: Finding My Way Back to Faith," due June 16, traces the vice president's journey from atheism to Catholicism.

stories.title: Hegseth calls upcoming days 'decisive' in Iran war
stories.description: The defense secretary pointed to mass desertions and degraded Iranian military capabilities as signs the conflict is nearing a turning point.

stories.title: Trump warns European allies after Italy, France block US military access
stories.description: Italy denied US bombers use of a Sicilian base, and France blocked Israeli overflights carrying American weapons, deepening a NATO rift over the Iran war.

stories.title: Global banks turn to Chinese stocks as Iran war roils markets
stories.description: Reuters reports major banks are treating Chinese equities as a safe haven, with renewable energy stocks surging even as global indexes tumble.

stories.title: Australia, Indonesia probe tech giants over under-16 bans
stories.description: Both countries are investigating major platforms for failing to enforce social media age restrictions, with fines and sanctions on the table.

stories.title: Noem's final day at DHS arrives amid IG probe, spending scandal
stories.description: Markwayne Mullin takes over as secretary while investigators examine contracts and lavish ad spending tied to Noem and adviser Corey Lewandowski.

stories.title: Data centers warm nearby land by up to 16°F, Cambridge study finds
stories.description: Satellite data from roughly 6,000 facilities worldwide shows AI data centers create "heat islands" affecting an estimated 340 million people.

stories.title: Pentagon denies Hegseth sought defense investments before Iran war
stories.description: The Financial Times reported that Hegseth's broker sought to invest millions in a defense ETF weeks before the Feb. 28 strike on Iran.

stories.title: Trump unveils 47-story skyscraper design for Miami presidential library
stories.description: The towering glass tower would break sharply from the low-slung, campus-style buildings that have defined presidential libraries for decades.

stories.title: NYU closes Abu Dhabi campus after Iran threatens US universities
stories.description: The IRGC declared American university campuses across the Gulf "legitimate targets," setting a Monday morning deadline for Washington to condemn strikes on Iranian schools.

stories.title: Health groups sue EPA over mercury rule rollback for coal plants
stories.description: A coalition of two dozen organizations filed suit in D.C. Circuit court, arguing the repeal of tighter emissions limits endangers children and communities.

stories.title: US struck Isfahan ammo depot with bunker busters
stories.description: Trump shared footage of the massive secondary explosions on Truth Social as the month-old U.S.-Israeli campaign surpasses 15,000 targets struck.

stories.title: Zelensky says Russia exploiting Iran war to fund its fight against Ukraine
stories.description: In an exclusive interview, the Ukrainian president accused Moscow of sharing satellite imagery with Iran and profiting from loosened oil sanctions.

stories.title: FBI labels Temple Isreal attack Hezbollah-inspired terrorism
stories.description: The bureau said Ayman Ghazali planned the March 12 ramming and shooting at Temple Israel to "kill as many" people as possible.

stories.title: Iran says ambassador will stay in Beirut despite Lebanon's expulsion order
stories.description: Tehran confirmed Monday its envoy remains in Lebanon past the March 29 departure deadline, deepening a diplomatic standoff backed by Hezbollah.

stories.title: Ukraine says Russia recruited 27,000 from 135 countries
stories.description: Ukrainian intelligence released data identifying 27,407 foreign nationals recruited into Russia's armed forces from 135 countries.

stories.title: Manhattan prosecutors probe insider trading on prediction markets
stories.description: The SDNY recently met with Polymarket to discuss how fraud and insider trading laws apply to suspicious bets on the fast-growing platforms.

stories.title: Israel Knesset passes death penalty law for Palestinians
stories.description: Military courts must now impose a mandatory death sentence for terrorism-related killings, with executions by hanging within 90 days of sentencing.

stories.title: NYT identifies untested U.S. missile in deadly Iran civilian strike
stories.description: A New York Times investigation found the Pentagon used a never-before-deployed Precision Strike Missile in a Feb. 28 strike that killed at least 21 people.

stories.title: Trump floats asking Arab states to help pay for Iran war
stories.description: The White House said Monday the president is interested in having Gulf allies share the financial burden of the conflict, now in its fifth week.

stories.title: Washington governor signs millionaires tax into law
stories.description: The 9.9% levy on household income above $1 million is the state's first personal income tax in nearly a century.

top.title: Sen. Lee urges Trump to recall Congress over DHS shutdown
top.description: The Utah Republican wants the president to invoke a constitutional power unused since 1948 to end the record 49-day DHS funding lapse.

top.title: S&P 500 caps five-week losing streak amid Iran war and jobs surprise
top.description: U.S. stocks closed Thursday slightly lower as oil topped $110 a barrel and a stronger-than-expected March jobs report dimmed hopes for rate cuts.

top.title: Brent crude hits $141 as Hormuz closure fuels worst energy crisis since 1970s
top.description: Oil prices surged to their highest level since 2008 this week as analysts warn strategic reserves will be exhausted by mid-April without a reopening of the strait.

top.title: Iran launches drone strikes on US bases in UAE, Kuwait
top.description: The Arash-2 drone attacks mark a further escalation as the US-Israeli war against Iran enters its sixth week.

top.title: Pope Leo urges world not to 'grow numb' to war at Easter vigil
top.description: The first American pope led his first Easter Vigil at St. Peter's Basilica, calling on Catholics to resist paralysis and work for peace.

top.title: Artemis II crew prepares for Monday lunar flyby
top.description: On their fourth day in space, the four astronauts are more than halfway to the Moon and readying for a record-breaking flyby.

top.title: US wage growth hits slowest pace since 2021 as Iran war stokes inflation fears
top.description: Annual earnings growth for production workers fell to 3.4% in March, well below the 4.2% inflation rate the OECD projects for 2026.

top.title: WHO warns of humanitarian crisis as Iran hospitals come under attack
top.description: WHO has verified over 20 attacks on healthcare in Iran since March 1, as strikes continue to cripple medical infrastructure across the country.

top.title: White House calls press lid as Trump health rumors spread online
top.description: Unverified speculation surged after the president's schedule showed only "Executive Time" and no briefing on the Iran military crisis.

top.title: U.N. Security Council postpones vote on Strait of Hormuz resolution to next week
top.description: The Bahrain-backed measure was watered down to permit only defensive actions after Russia, China, and France opposed authorizing military force.

top.title: Russia, Ukraine trade deadly strikes as Zelenskyy meets Erdogan
top.description: Five people were killed and dozens wounded in a Russian drone strike on a market in Nikopol as Zelenskyy held peace-focused talks in Istanbul.

top.title: Israel readying strikes on Iran's energy sites, awaits US approval
top.description: A senior Israeli defense official said strikes could come within the next week, as Trump's April 6 deadline for Iran to reopen the Strait of Hormuz nears.

top.title: Zelenskyy, Erdogan agree on new security steps at Istanbul talks
top.description: Ukraine will share wartime expertise and technology with Turkey as part of a broader cooperation deal reached Saturday.

top.title: Displaced Lebanese Shiites face sectarian hostility amid war
top.description: Over 1.1 million people have fled Israeli strikes, but many Shiite families encounter discrimination, inflated rents, and eviction demands in host communities.

top.title: Trump administration appeals to resume White House ballroom work
top.description: The emergency motion argues the halted $400 million project has left the executive mansion "open and exposed," posing security risks.

top.title: Israel hits Beirut, Tyre as Lebanon war enters second month
top.description: Strikes damaged a hospital in Tyre and targeted Iranian Quds Force positions in Beirut as the conflict's death toll surpasses 1,368.

top.title: India confirms Iranian oil purchases amid Hormuz crisis
top.description: The petroleum ministry said refiners have secured crude and LPG supplies, marking India's first Iranian oil shipment since 2019.

top.title: Trump's $1.5T defense budget draws fire from both parties
top.description: Sen. Collins joined Democrats in criticizing the proposal, citing insufficient Navy funding and cuts to domestic programs she called "proven."

top.title: ICE arrests Soleimani's niece after Rubio revokes green cards
top.description: Federal agents detained Hamideh Soleimani Afshar and her daughter in Los Angeles after the secretary of state terminated their permanent resident status.

top.title: Iran authorizes humanitarian ships through Strait of Hormuz
top.description: The directive permits vessels carrying essential goods and livestock feed to reach Iranian ports, over five weeks into an effective blockade of the critical waterway.

top.title: Trump gives Iran 48 hours as strikes hit petrochemical sites
top.description: The president reaffirmed his April 6 deadline for Tehran to reopen the Strait of Hormuz, warning "all hell" would follow if it does not comply.

top.title: Witkoff and Kushner plan first visit to Kyiv after Easter
top.description: Ukraine's Budanov says the U.S. envoys are expected to travel to Kyiv after Orthodox Easter on April 12, their first trip to the capital.

top.title: Thailand probes fuel hoarding as Southeast Asia's energy crisis deepens
top.description: Five weeks after Iran closed the Strait of Hormuz, Southeast Asian nations face mounting fuel shortages, soaring costs, and emergency rationing measures.

top.title: Iran rapidly repairing bombed missile sites, U.S. intel says
top.description: Intelligence assessments show Iranian forces restoring struck bunkers within hours, even as Trump projects the war will end in weeks.

top.title: Erdogan tells NATO chief Iran war has caused 'geostrategic impasse'
top.description: The Turkish president urged the international community to intensify efforts to end the conflict during a Saturday phone call with Mark Rutte.

top.title: Slovak, Hungarian PMs demand EU lift Russia energy sanctions
top.description: The two leaders cited Europe's deepening energy crisis, driven by the Iran conflict, as reason to restore Russian oil and gas flows immediately.

top.title: Former Carter aide calls US a 'rogue nation' over Iran war
top.description: Gary Sick, who served on the National Security Council under three presidents, warned the US is "flouting the laws of war" as the conflict enters its sixth week.

top.title: Hamas accuses Israel of fabricating threats to justify Gaza ceasefire violations
top.description: Since the October ceasefire took effect, Israeli attacks have killed at least 713 Palestinians, according to Gaza's Health Ministry.

top.title: Gaza ceasefire talks end in Cairo with plans to resume next week
top.description: Hamas called for the immediate formation of a transitional committee to govern Gaza and oversee reconstruction under the ceasefire's second phase.

top.title: Iran downs 2 US warplanes, undercutting Trump's victory claims
top.description: The shootdowns mark the first time manned U.S. aircraft have been brought down since the war began, with one crew member still missing.

top.title: Interception debris strikes Oracle building in Dubai amid Iran conflict
top.description: Oracle is among 18 U.S. tech firms that Iran's Revolutionary Guard declared "legitimate targets" in late March.

top.title: Iran says it 'never refused' Pakistan-hosted talks with US
top.description: Iran's foreign minister pushed back on reports that Tehran rejected mediation, even as Pakistan denied its diplomatic initiative had stalled.

top.title: Iran vows to 'finish' any US ground operation as war nears 6th week
top.description: A diplomatic source said Tehran would determine the outcome of any American ground incursion, adding that no negotiations are underway.

top.title: Turkey confirms second ship crosses Strait of Hormuz
top.description: Omani tankers also charted a new southern route bypassing Iran's controlled corridor as transit volumes inch up from wartime lows.

top.title: UAE intercepts 79 Iranian projectiles as strikes batter Gulf infrastructure
top.description: Saturday's wave brought total interceptions since Feb. 28 to nearly 2,700, as key energy and industrial sites sustain growing damage.

top.title: UAE rejects Zarif's peace proposal, demands Iran compensate Gulf states
top.description: Dr. Anwar Gargash called the former Iranian FM's plan "fundamentally flawed" for ignoring Tehran's missile and drone attacks on Arab neighbors.

top.title: Trump weighs broader cabinet shake-up as war frustration mounts
top.description: Lutnick, Gabbard, and Chavez-DeRemer are among those on thin ice as Trump's approval hits a term low amid the Iran conflict.

top.title: Ukrainian drone strikes kill one in Taganrog, hit chemical plants deep in Russia
top.description: Overnight attacks targeted southern Russia's port city and industrial sites over 750 km from the front lines, leaving four in critical condition.

top.title: Five EU finance ministers demand windfall tax on energy firms
top.description: Germany, Italy, Spain, Portugal, and Austria urged the European Commission to tax energy companies' excess profits from the Iran war price surge.

top.title: Trump's FY2027 budget boosts defense 42%, flat-funds SSA
top.description: The $2.2 trillion proposal cuts nondefense spending by $73 billion while avoiding reforms to Social Security and Medicare as insolvency looms.

top.title: US pulled 1,500 sailors from Bahrain naval base after Iranian strikes
top.description: Relief groups have distributed over $1 million to evacuated families who fled with little more than backpacks, NPR reported.

top.title: North Korea razes villages near key missile launch site
top.description: Satellite imagery shows hundreds of buildings demolished near the Sohae station, pointing to an expansion of the facility amid an accelerating weapons buildup.

top.title: GOP cracks widen over Iran war as midterms loom
top.description: Rising gas prices, casualties, and an approaching War Powers Resolution deadline fuel Republican defections from Trump on Iran.

top.title: US Embassy urges Americans to leave Lebanon over Iran university threats
top.description: The Beirut embassy warned Friday that Iran and allied militias may target universities in Lebanon amid ongoing airstrikes and rocket fire.

top.title: French, Japanese ships cross Hormuz in first Western transits since Iran war
top.description: CMA CGM and Mitsui O.S.K. Lines vessels broke through Iran's selective blockade this week, though roughly 3,000 ships remain stranded globally.

top.title: Doctors Without Borders confirms Gaza supply shutdown since January
top.description: Doctors Without Borders warned that Israeli restrictions have left nearly half of essential chronic medications critically low across Gaza.

top.title: Iran drone strike on Riyadh embassy far worse than disclosed
top.description: Multiple embassy floors were heavily damaged and sections remain "unrecoverable," according to current and former U.S. officials.

top.title: Earthquake kills 8 in Afghanistan, tremors felt across South Asia
top.description: A magnitude 5.9 quake centered in the Hindu Kush region caused a house to collapse on the outskirts of Kabul, killing an entire family.

top.title: Three charged over arson attack on Jewish ambulances in London
top.description: The defendants are due to appear at Westminster Magistrates' Court on Saturday after being arrested on April 1.

top.title: Pentagon logs 365 troops wounded in Operation Epic Fury
top.description: The Defense Department added the Iran campaign to its official casualty database on Friday, providing the most detailed public accounting of the war's toll.

top.title: War silences Good Friday in Jerusalem as Pope Leo XIV calls for peace
top.description: Only a handful of worshippers walked the Via Dolorosa as Israeli restrictions blocked access to holy sites amid the U.S.-Israel war on Iran.

top.title: Trump signs memo ordering back pay for all DHS employees
top.description: The directive extends relief beyond TSA workers to roughly 220,000 federal employees who have gone without pay during the 48-day partial shutdown.

top.title: US intel finds Iran unlikely to negotiate on Hormuz blockade
top.description: Intelligence assessments say Tehran sees no need to comply with U.S. demands as the April 6 deadline to reopen the strait approaches.

top.title: Justice Alito was secretly hospitalized in March, court confirms
top.description: The 76-year-old Supreme Court justice was treated for dehydration after falling ill at a Federalist Society dinner in Philadelphia.

top.title: New acting AG Blanche says Epstein files should not be DOJ priority
top.description: Democrats and Republicans vow to enforce a subpoena requiring fired AG Pam Bondi to testify on April 14 about unreleased Epstein documents.

top.title: UN says Iran war is accelerating global renewable energy shift
top.description: IRENA reports renewables hit a record 49.4% of global capacity in 2025 as the energy crisis pushes countries toward domestic clean power.

top.title: Oracle faces backlash over H-1B visa filings amid mass layoffs
top.description: Critics call it a "slap in the face" that the company filed over 3,100 foreign-worker visa petitions in the same period it cut up to 30,000 jobs.

top.title: 23 state AGs sue to block Trump's mail voting order
top.description: The coalition filed suit Friday in Boston, arguing the executive order unconstitutionally strips states of their authority to administer elections.

top.title: Satellite images confirm Iran damaged $136M US radar in Saudi Arabia
top.description: New imagery shows charring and missing sections on a THAAD radar antenna struck at Prince Sultan Air Base on March 1.

top.title: IDF says disarming Hezbollah 'not a goal' of Lebanon campaign
top.description: A senior military official's remarks signal a shift from earlier war aims after one month of fighting that has hit over 3,500 targets.

top.title: Trump budget seeks to privatize TSA screening
top.description: The fiscal 2027 proposal pairs a $1.5 trillion defense request with a 10% cut to nondefense spending, including a push to shift airport security to private contractors.

top.title: US adds 178,000 jobs in March, far beating forecasts
top.description: The unemployment rate fell to 4.3% as the labor market rebounded sharply from February's loss of 92,000 jobs.

top.title: Macron calls for 'coalition of independence' against US and China
top.description: Speaking in Seoul, the French president urged democratic middle powers to resist becoming "vassals of two hegemonic powers."

top.title: Judge reaffirms block on DOJ subpoenas targeting Fed's Powell
top.description: The ruling sets up a likely appeal that could delay Trump's push to replace Powell with Kevin Warsh before Powell's term expires May 15.

top.title: China's Tianlong-3 rocket fails on maiden flight
top.description: The privately built rocket, designed to rival SpaceX's Falcon 9, suffered a mid-flight anomaly launching from the Gobi Desert.

top.title: Georgia legislature adjourns without resolving voting machine standoff
top.description: Conflicting state laws take effect July 1, leaving all 159 counties without a clear path for conducting November's midterm elections.

top.title: Russia holds nuclear missile drills as Trump threatens NATO exit
top.description: Moscow showcased Yars ICBMs in Siberian exercises as European leaders warned Trump's withdrawal threats serve Putin's interests.

top.title: Environmental groups sue over Gulf drilling's ESA exemption
top.description: Conservation groups filed lawsuits challenging the Trump administration's unprecedented decision to exempt all Gulf oil and gas operations from the Endangered Species Act.

top.title: Global food prices jump for second straight month as Middle East war disrupts supply chains
top.description: The FAO Food Price Index rose sharply in March, driven by surging energy and fertilizer costs linked to the conflict in Iran.

top.title: Trump names Vance 'fraud czar,' targeting Democratic states
top.description: The announcement follows a major hospice fraud crackdown in Los Angeles that led to 11 charges and 221 provider suspensions.

top.title: Min Aung Hlaing wins parliamentary vote to become Myanmar president
top.description: The former coup leader won a bicameral vote Friday after resigning as military chief, completing a five-year transition to nominal civilian rule.

top.title: South Korea, France elevate ties to strategic partnership
top.description: The two leaders signed 11 MOUs covering nuclear energy, critical minerals, and defense after summit talks in Seoul on Friday.

top.title: Trump says Israel will halt Iran war on his orders
top.description: In a TIME interview, the president claimed personal authority over the five-week-old conflict, saying of Israel: "They'll do what I tell them."

top.title: Shell, TotalEnergies and BP eye majority stake in Gulf deepwater field
top.description: Beacon Offshore Energy and HEQ Deepwater have launched a sale of their combined 51% stake in the Shenandoah ultra-deepwater project, sources say.

top.title: Hegseth forces out Army chief of staff Gen. Randy George
top.description: The dismissal caps a turbulent week after Hegseth blocked promotions of Black and female officers, deepening rifts with Army leadership.

top.title: McConnell collapses at Senate Republican luncheon
top.description: The 84-year-old Kentucky senator required medical assistance Thursday, the latest in a string of health scares during his final term.

top.title: IRGC launches fresh strikes on steel, aluminum plants across Middle East
top.description: Iran announced a new wave of attacks on April 2 targeting U.S.-linked steel and aluminum facilities, escalating a conflict that has sent prices soaring.

top.title: Vance to visit Hungary, meet with Orban days before election
top.description: The White House confirmed the vice president will travel to Budapest on April 7-8 as polls show Orban trailing his challenger by double digits.

top.title: Dem senators urge SEC, Pentagon to probe insider trading
top.description: Sens. Warner and Schiff called on regulators Thursday to investigate suspicious trades ahead of major Trump administration policy moves.

top.title: Boeser's hat trick lifts last-place Canucks past Avalanche 8-6
top.description: Nathan MacKinnon scored his 50th goal of the season, but Vancouver rallied to snap a six-game skid in a wild game in Denver.

top.title: GCC chief condemns Iran strikes in first-ever UN Council address
top.description: Jassim Albudaiwi warned the world is at a "dangerous crossroads" and urged the Security Council to enforce Resolution 2817.

top.title: OpenAI acquires TBPN, Silicon Valley's hit tech talk show
top.description: The deal extends OpenAI's reach into media, marking a departure from its recent string of developer-focused startup acquisitions.

top.title: IRGC claims new strikes on Amazon data center in Bahrain
top.description: Iran's military says it targeted the AWS cloud computing center as part of the 90th wave of Operation True Promise 4.

top.title: Blue Owl caps fund redemptions after $5.4B in withdrawal requests
top.description: Investors sought to pull over 40% of one fund's shares, the worst hit yet in a wave of redemption pressure sweeping the private credit sector.

top.title: US jobless claims fall to 202,000, defying Iran war headwinds
top.description: Weekly filings dropped well below forecasts even as the conflict's energy shock weighs on the broader economy and hiring outlook.

top.title: Magnitude 7.4 earthquake kills one in Indonesia, triggers brief tsunami warning
top.description: The undersea quake toppled buildings across northern Indonesia and generated small tsunami waves before warnings were lifted hours later.

top.title: Iraq's oil exports halt as Hormuz closure cuts output 70%
top.description: Southern Iraq's oil production has plunged from 3.1 million to 900,000 barrels per day, with exports completely stopped due to the Strait of Hormuz shutdown.

top.title: One year after Liberation Day, tariff refunds loom
top.description: April 2 marks the anniversary of Trump's sweeping tariffs, most of which were struck down by the Supreme Court, with $165 billion in refunds still pending.

top.title: Syracuse University to close or pause 93 programs
top.description: The provost said 93 of roughly 460 degree programs and certificates will be eliminated, affecting about 20% of offerings and 258 students.

top.title: CDC pauses rabies, monkeypox, and other disease testing amid staff cuts
top.description: The agency suspended more than two dozen types of diagnostic testing, citing a routine review as staffing fell by up to 25%.

top.title: Trump leaves Supreme Court early, calls birthright citizenship 'stupid'
top.description: The president became the first to attend oral arguments, but justices signaled skepticism of his order to end birthright citizenship.

top.title: US troop housing in Gulf hotels may violate laws of war
top.description: Legal experts say relocating troops from damaged bases to civilian hotels blurs the line between military and civilian targets under the Geneva Conventions.

top.title: Nepal charges 32 in $20M Everest helicopter rescue scam
top.description: Guides allegedly poisoned trekkers with baking soda to trigger fake evacuations, then forged medical and flight records to defraud insurers.

top.title: DHS scraps Noem's $100K contract approval rule, unblocking FEMA relief
top.description: Secretary Markwayne Mullin's first major action reverses a policy that delayed over 1,000 FEMA contracts and billions in disaster aid.

top.title: Mangione's state and federal trials both delayed
top.description: A New York judge pushed the state murder trial to September, hours after a federal judge moved jury selection to October, compressing an already tight timeline.

top.title: Russia vows continued aid to Cuba after first oil shipment in three months
top.description: Moscow called Cuba its "closest ally in the Caribbean" as Havana faces a deepening energy crisis under a U.S. fuel blockade.

top.title: Judge lets ABA lawsuit over Trump's law firm orders proceed
top.description: A federal judge denied the DOJ's motion to dismiss, ruling the ABA plausibly showed Trump's executive orders suppressed free expression.

top.title: ICE says it bought phone-hacking spyware to fight fentanyl
top.description: The agency's disclosure to lawmakers is its first public justification for a controversial $2 million contract with Israeli spyware firm Paragon Solutions.

top.title: US lifts sanctions on Venezuela's acting president Rodríguez
top.description: The Treasury Department delisted Rodríguez, who had been sanctioned since 2018, as Washington deepens ties with Caracas after Maduro's capture.

top.title: Appeals court blocks HUD from overhauling homelessness grants
top.description: The 1st Circuit upheld an injunction preserving billions in permanent housing funds, rejecting the Trump administration's bid to impose new restrictions.

top.title: Seizing Iran's uranium by force would risk casualties, experts warn
top.description: A military operation to secure Iran's 440.9 kg of near-weapons-grade uranium could require 1,000 troops and face radiation hazards, former officials say.

top.title: Shell in advanced talks to develop major gas fields in Venezuela
top.description: The energy giant is pursuing four offshore areas near Trinidad with combined reserves of roughly 20 trillion cubic feet, according to Reuters.

top.title: Iran's Pezeshkian tells Americans his country 'harbors no enmity' toward them
top.description: The open letter bypasses the Trump administration to appeal directly to the U.S. public as the military conflict enters its fifth week.

top.title: AOC pledges to oppose all U.S. arms funding for Israel
top.description: The commitment, made at a DSA endorsement forum Tuesday night, reverses her July 2025 vote to protect Iron Dome funding.

top.title: Israel kills Hezbollah's Southern Front commander in Beirut naval strike
top.description: Hajj Yusuf Ismail Hashem, a 40-year Hezbollah veteran who led operations in southern Lebanon, died in an overnight strike on the Jnah district.

top.title: Macron reaffirms France won't join US-Israel war on Iran
top.description: Speaking in Tokyo, Macron pushed back on Trump's accusation that France blocked U.S. military flights, saying France was never consulted.

top.title: South Korea exports hit record $86B as global factories stockpile amid Iran war
top.description: Semiconductor shipments surged over 150%, while euro zone and U.S. factory activity beat forecasts as firms panic-bought materials ahead of further disruptions.

top.title: Qubits needed to crack encryption fall 200-fold in under a year
top.description: Three papers have slashed the estimated quantum computing power needed to break RSA-2048 from 20 million qubits to under 100,000, accelerating the threat to modern encryption.

top.title: Trump to address nation on Iran as war enters day 33
top.description: The president said Tuesday that U.S. forces would be "leaving soon," predicting the conflict could end within two to three weeks.

top.title: Macron and Takaichi align on Hormuz crisis, rare earths in Tokyo summit
top.description: The first French presidential visit to Japan in over a decade was dominated by the Middle East conflict, energy security, and reducing reliance on China for critical minerals.

top.title: Parker Solar Probe data challenges models of Sun's magnetic engine
top.description: Protons and heavy ions behave differently during solar magnetic reconnection, contradicting models that assumed uniform particle acceleration.

top.title: Conservative justices question Trump birthright order
top.description: Trump became the first sitting president to attend Supreme Court oral arguments as justices from both wings challenged his executive order.

top.title: USTR flags Canadian alcohol bans, 'Buy Canadian' as trade barriers
top.description: The annual report demands American liquor "immediately and permanently" return to provincial shelves ahead of the USMCA's first formal review in July.

top.title: New papers slash qubit estimates for codebreaking
top.description: The March 31 papers sharply cut projected quantum resources needed to crack elliptic curve encryption, intensifying pressure to adopt post-quantum safeguards.

top.title: ICE agents to remain at airports indefinitely, Duffy says
top.description: Transportation secretary says deployment will continue as TSA struggles to rebuild staffing after the DHS shutdown left officers unpaid for weeks.

top.title: Forest Service to move headquarters to Salt Lake City in sweeping overhaul
top.description: The USDA announced the agency's nine regional offices will close and be replaced by 15 state directors under a new organizational model.

top.title: China and Pakistan propose ceasefire as Chinese ships transit Hormuz
top.description: Beijing confirmed three Chinese vessels crossed the strait after coordinating with Tehran, as China and Pakistan unveiled a five-point peace plan.

top.title: DHS pauses warehouse purchases for immigrant detention
top.description: New Secretary Mullin is reviewing the $38.3 billion detention expansion program launched by his predecessor, Kristi Noem.

top.title: Netanyahu says Israel destroying Iran's weapons capability
top.description: In a Passover eve address, the prime minister said Israel had inflicted "10 plagues" on Iran and its regional proxy networks.

top.title: Syria vows to stay out of US-Israeli war on Iran unless attacked
top.description: President Ahmed al-Sharaa declared neutrality in a London speech, saying Syria has "had enough war" as the conflict enters its second month.

top.title: Rubio directs US embassies to use X and military psyops against propaganda
top.description: The diplomatic cable instructs staff worldwide to coordinate with Pentagon psychological operations and leverage Elon Musk's platform.

top.title: Judge blocks deal that would let churches endorse candidates
top.description: A federal judge in Texas rejected a proposed IRS settlement that would have exempted houses of worship from the Johnson Amendment's ban on political endorsements.

top.title: Explosions rock Damascus as Israel intercepts Iranian missiles over Syria
top.description: Syrian state TV reported blasts across the capital on Tuesday as Israeli air defenses shot down Iranian projectiles crossing Syrian airspace.

top.title: FDA moves to allow banned peptides backed by RFK Jr.
top.description: The agency is expected to reclassify more than a dozen injectable peptides restricted in 2023, restoring access through compounding pharmacies.

top.title: Judge orders UPenn to hand over Jewish employee records
top.description: The ruling gives Penn until May 1 to comply with the subpoena but bars disclosure of individuals' ties to specific Jewish organizations.

top.title: Majority disapprove of Trump's signature on dollar bills, YouGov poll finds
top.description: The survey found 48% strongly oppose replacing the U.S. Treasurer's signature with the president's on new paper currency.

top.title: Bipartisan senators introduce bill to boost Taiwan drone production
top.description: The legislation would create a U.S.-Taiwan working group to co-produce drones and build supply chains independent from China.

top.title: Supreme Court strikes down Colorado conversion therapy ban in 8-1 ruling
top.description: The decision in Chiles v. Salazar requires courts to apply heightened First Amendment scrutiny to laws restricting talk therapy for minors.

top.title: Judge permanently blocks Trump order defunding NPR, PBS
top.description: U.S. District Judge Randolph Moss ruled the executive order violated the First Amendment by punishing the outlets over speech the president dislikes.

top.title: Vance to publish book on Catholic faith while in office
top.description: "Communion: Finding My Way Back to Faith," due June 16, traces the vice president's journey from atheism to Catholicism.

top.title: Hegseth calls upcoming days 'decisive' in Iran war
top.description: The defense secretary pointed to mass desertions and degraded Iranian military capabilities as signs the conflict is nearing a turning point.

top.title: Trump warns European allies after Italy, France block US military access
top.description: Italy denied US bombers use of a Sicilian base, and France blocked Israeli overflights carrying American weapons, deepening a NATO rift over the Iran war.

top.title: Global banks turn to Chinese stocks as Iran war roils markets
top.description: Reuters reports major banks are treating Chinese equities as a safe haven, with renewable energy stocks surging even as global indexes tumble.

top.title: Australia, Indonesia probe tech giants over under-16 bans
top.description: Both countries are investigating major platforms for failing to enforce social media age restrictions, with fines and sanctions on the table.

top.title: Noem's final day at DHS arrives amid IG probe, spending scandal
top.description: Markwayne Mullin takes over as secretary while investigators examine contracts and lavish ad spending tied to Noem and adviser Corey Lewandowski.

top.title: Data centers warm nearby land by up to 16°F, Cambridge study finds
top.description: Satellite data from roughly 6,000 facilities worldwide shows AI data centers create "heat islands" affecting an estimated 340 million people.

top.title: Pentagon denies Hegseth sought defense investments before Iran war
top.description: The Financial Times reported that Hegseth's broker sought to invest millions in a defense ETF weeks before the Feb. 28 strike on Iran.

top.title: Trump unveils 47-story skyscraper design for Miami presidential library
top.description: The towering glass tower would break sharply from the low-slung, campus-style buildings that have defined presidential libraries for decades.

top.title: NYU closes Abu Dhabi campus after Iran threatens US universities
top.description: The IRGC declared American university campuses across the Gulf "legitimate targets," setting a Monday morning deadline for Washington to condemn strikes on Iranian schools.

top.title: Health groups sue EPA over mercury rule rollback for coal plants
top.description: A coalition of two dozen organizations filed suit in D.C. Circuit court, arguing the repeal of tighter emissions limits endangers children and communities.

top.title: US struck Isfahan ammo depot with bunker busters
top.description: Trump shared footage of the massive secondary explosions on Truth Social as the month-old U.S.-Israeli campaign surpasses 15,000 targets struck.

top.title: Zelensky says Russia exploiting Iran war to fund its fight against Ukraine
top.description: In an exclusive interview, the Ukrainian president accused Moscow of sharing satellite imagery with Iran and profiting from loosened oil sanctions.

top.title: FBI labels Temple Isreal attack Hezbollah-inspired terrorism
top.description: The bureau said Ayman Ghazali planned the March 12 ramming and shooting at Temple Israel to "kill as many" people as possible.

top.title: Iran says ambassador will stay in Beirut despite Lebanon's expulsion order
top.description: Tehran confirmed Monday its envoy remains in Lebanon past the March 29 departure deadline, deepening a diplomatic standoff backed by Hezbollah.

top.title: Ukraine says Russia recruited 27,000 from 135 countries
top.description: Ukrainian intelligence released data identifying 27,407 foreign nationals recruited into Russia's armed forces from 135 countries.

top.title: Manhattan prosecutors probe insider trading on prediction markets
top.description: The SDNY recently met with Polymarket to discuss how fraud and insider trading laws apply to suspicious bets on the fast-growing platforms.

top.title: Israel Knesset passes death penalty law for Palestinians
top.description: Military courts must now impose a mandatory death sentence for terrorism-related killings, with executions by hanging within 90 days of sentencing.

top.title: NYT identifies untested U.S. missile in deadly Iran civilian strike
top.description: A New York Times investigation found the Pentagon used a never-before-deployed Precision Strike Missile in a Feb. 28 strike that killed at least 21 people.

top.title: Trump floats asking Arab states to help pay for Iran war
top.description: The White House said Monday the president is interested in having Gulf allies share the financial burden of the conflict, now in its fifth week.

top.title: Washington governor signs millionaires tax into law
top.description: The 9.9% levy on household income above $1 million is the state's first personal income tax in nearly a century.
